author: Ricardo
content: Is it necessary to vote to earn sundae tokens? Also, after the pools are selected you can an join one that have been selected and earn sundae tokens even if you were on another pool (not selected) before?

author: Peter
content:
No, you don't need to vote to earn the tokens but it really helps some of the small pools get into the process. Otherwise, the process simply centralises the process into the large popular pools.

Yes after the pools are selected in the vote, simply pick one of those pools to stake into for ADA rewards and Sundae tokens. You will have to leave your current pool and join the new  SundaeSwap pool.

Firstly you can just stake your Ada to a pool. That will get you your Ada rewards and Sundae tokens. Hopefully we, ADAOZ, is in that list. 

Tokens are distributed every epoch for 25 days. 


You will need to interact with a smart contract to unlock it.

content: You're welcome! Hang around. We've got quite a lot of interesting DeFi content coming up.

author: Ryan
content: Can I use ada lite to delegate my Cardano to receive sundaeswap rewards??

author: Peter
content:
It can be used for staking, BUT it doesn't show your native tokens. So you can stake there but you won't be able to see your $SUNDAE tokens.

You can use your ADALite mnemonic seed phrase and restore that in Yoroi or Daedalus to see your Sundae tokens and access them.

To trade the Sundae tokens, you will need to use a web3 wallet such as Nami or Yoroi Dapp Connector.

(I deleted the repeated comments btw and approved your account to post more)

author: Amanda
content: I voted for two ISO selected but I'm not skating with them. Should I change my delegation if I want to receive my awards or I will receive my awards just for had voted?

author: Peter
content:
Well, you will need your ADA in a web3 wallet like Nami or Yoroi with the Dapp connector to be able to interact with the DEX. At that point, you will be able to buy more Sundae tokens or sell the tokens that you gain from staking.

If the wallet that you have staked is a web3 style wallet that will allow you to connect to the website then you're set and ready to go.

author: Peter
content:
Hey Amanda, firstly, there were no rewards for voting on this. Purely just being able to participate in the process of the first DEX on Cardano.

Secondly, the ISO hasn't quite started yet. So there is no need to rush your delegation just yet. It may be a week or two away before it all happens. Make sure you subscribe to the podcast or the YouTube channel to stay up to date.

author: Phil
content:
Thank you for the article, most of my questions are cleared and getting to understand the ada projects.
Have a question, sorry if it is already answered else where, will I still get the Sundaetokens if I stake to the new pool before/on the day of launch or may be next day of the Sundaeswap ISO launch? I have seen some where that it should be delegated before the ISO launch, but not exactly sure. Could you please help me understand on this?

In general the first epoch will be delayed correct? in that case, will i still be eligible for Sundaetokens?

Also, can we move/sell the Sundae Tokens after every epoch?

author: Peter
content: From what I understand, if you're using Daedalus, you will be able to receive your Sundae rewards when you interact with a contract at the 5 day period BUT you won't be able interact with the website to swap the Sundae for ADA or vice versa

author: Peter
content:
Hey Phil:
if I stake to the new pool before/on the day of launch or may be next day of the Sundaeswap ISO launch? I have seen some where that it should be delegated before the ISO launch, but not exactly sure. Could you please help me understand on this?
The snapshot is take at the epoch change over. Epoch being 5 day period. From what I understand, you will need to be in the pool before that initial launch snapshot. Since they haven't officially announced a date yet, there is no need to move to one of the pools. Just be ready to when you can. You'll have at least 5 days to do so.
In general the first epoch will be delayed correct? in that case, will I still be eligible for Sundaetokens?
This isn't the same as ADA staking. They can scan and calculate the data on the blockchain-based on where you are staked and how much is there at the snapshot point. They will use that as the amount for sundae distribution. There is no locking and you can move your ADA in and out as long as you are there for the snapshot points at the end of each epoch.
Also, can we move/sell the Sundae Tokens after every epoch?
Yes, that's right, you are free to sell the sundae tokens or ADA as soon as you receive them and do as you please.

content: Yeh you're fine. You're actually a little too early, which is fine too but the previous pool that you left for the Sundae tokens may have benefitted more from your delegation than these large Sundae pools. With 74 pools to choose from, no one is going to miss out.

author: Peter
content: All good, you're really early. ISO hasn't started and the start date hasn't been annouced.

author: Peter
content:
At the moment with the current amount of ADA staked to the pools and the amount being distributed, the ratio is

1 ADA = 0.03 Sundae.

Once the ISO actually starts, that will be even lower and could be as low as 0.01 Sundae per ADA delegated.

author: Peter
content: Technically yes as they are a native Cardano wallet, but that wallet isn't connected in regards to the DEX

author: Peter
content:
Snapshot date has now been announced. 25th Jan.

You will need to either setup a new wallet and move the ADA to it and start delegating now or simply move an existing wallet to do the same thing.

There is a 2 epoch delay before the ADA rewards are calculated but Sundae are using the LIVE count and not active. The delay isn't that long but still long for ADA rewards.

author: Anthony
content: As a new single stake pool operator, ADAreactor(ARC), is there a chance to get picked for this project at a later time? Will there be another round of voting? Or, will there be an application process to request to be added to stake pool list?

author: Peter
content:
In the future you will be able to apply for a Scooper license.

There are other DEXes that are using a similar model so its worth looking into each one of them and seeing which one looks good to you.

I do not ever want to go through the voting process that SundaeSwap did ever again. I didn't sleep for days and the pools with the most ADA won in the end. Very hard to compete but I'm still glad we made it to the top 40.

author: Case
content:
hey Peter - great video and thanks for all your help at getting back to everyone with questions!

I have a quick one - so I've delegated to a Sundae ISO pool via Yoroi and I'm just wondering once the 25 days is up for the token allocation, and you complete whatever smart contract exists to receive these sundae swap tokens, do they get sent into your Yoroi wallet?  And then theoretically would you be able to transfer them over to a Nami wallet since Yoroi and SundaeSwap aren't currently compatible?

Just trying to figure out how you would get them in (via smart contract - even though Yoroi seems to be only ADA compatible) but also out of Yoroi wallet so you can actually start utilizing them on the SundaeSwap platform...

Thanks again for all your help!

author: Peter
content:
It's not a huge deal but spreading out the delegated ADA across many pools makes the network less centralised.

The more ADA some of the large multi pools have the more control over the network and influence they may hold.

When it gets above 15+ pools, and they're all not fully saturated then they're just manipulating the system for financial gain. (thanks how most see it). But if they have legitimately a good reason to have multiple full pools then that is another case.

If they have 2-3 and they're over 60% saturation, I'd say that's still healthy.

author: Peter
content:
Here, please use this:
https://poolpeek.com/#/sundaeiso

The websites are getting hammered ?

author: Peter
content:
Yes they get sent back to the address that triggered the claim process, assuming that is also the wallet that delegated for Sundae.

Yes you can then transfer them over.

Yoroi can actually accept and display native tokens and nfts too. If you don't have any you won't see the ASSET tab in the wallet.

Don't forget to subscribe and consider delegating when this is all over.

author: Hugo Knight
content:
Hey Peter, 

My sentiments have already been expressed by others above. Appreciate the article. 

Apart from Sundae Swap tokens being rewarded. What's the yield return APR? 
Or is this only for the ISO and then we withdraw our funds? 

The fixed fee of 340ADA is that per staker or for the entire pool? 

What does last luck epoch % mean?

Best
Hugo

author: Peter
content:
The APR for yield farming was as high as 300% which was quite high.

The Fixed fee is taken from the total rewards not one person. The entire pool.

Luck relates to how many blocks are assigned to the pool. If you're lucky, we get more, if we're unlucky we get less. The protocol tried to average out all pools to 100%.
